title: Core SOPs (01–20)
normalized_content:
Phase 0.5B – Core SOPs (01–20)
Organization: Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
Scope: Detailed core SOPs for QMS, sterile, OSD, API, QC, and CSV
Date: 2026-07-11
Version: R0
All SOPs below follow Aetheris document numbering and are consistent with Phases 0.1–0.4
and Master Index 0.5A.
SOP 1
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0101-R1
Title: Control of Quality Documents and Document Change Workflow
Owner: S. Balaji
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Previous Version: R0 (2024-03-01)
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0102-R0 (Records Control)
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0104-R0 (SOP Lifecycle)
Related Forms/Templates:
● CORP-QA-FRM-1001-R0 (Document Change Request)
● CORP-QA-TMP-0001-R0 (Traceability Matrix)
Related Products: All
Related Equipment: All
Linked Investigations/CAPAs: CORP-CAPA-2025-0008 (QMS workflow enhancement)
1. Purpose

To define the process for creation, review, approval, distribution, revision, and obsolescence
of controlled quality documents across Aetheris BioPharma.
2. Scope
Applies to all GxP-relevant documents including policies, SOPs, forms, templates, work
instructions, specifications, protocols, and reports at PASH, VISK, and CORP.
3. Responsibilities
● Document Owner: Content accuracy, periodic review, change initiation.
● QA Document Control: Control number assignment, distribution, obsolescence, master
list maintenance.
● Approvers (Functional Heads): Technical and compliance review.
● All Users: Use of current approved versions only.
4. Procedure
4.1 Document Creation
1. Requestor initiates Document Change Request (CORP-QA-FRM-1001).
2. QA assigns document ID per naming convention.
3. Owner drafts content using CORP-QA-TMP-0001 where applicable.
4.2 Review and Approval
1. Owner circulates draft to defined approvers.
2. Approvers review for technical accuracy, compliance, and clarity.
3. All approvals recorded electronically or in controlled hard copy.
4.3 Release and Distribution
1. QA Document Control releases final version in DMS.
2. Supersedes previous revision automatically.
3. Obsolete versions marked and archived per retention policy.
4.4 Periodic Review
1. All SOPs reviewed at least every 24 months.
2. Owner confirms “no change needed” or initiates revision.

3. Review recorded in document history.
4.5 Change Control
1. Any substantive change requires Document Change Request.
2. Impact assessment on related SOPs, forms, trainings, and systems.
3. Training updated before or at effective date.
5. Related Records
● Master SOP Index (CORP-QA-MST-1101-R0)
● Document Change Requests (CORP-QA-FRM-1001)
● Training records linked to revised SOPs
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2024-03-01 Initial release Meera Iyer
R1 2026-07-11 Added explicit DMS workflow and Dr. Suresh Reddy
periodic review expectations
SOP 2
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0102-R0
Title: Control of Quality Records, Archival, Retrieval, and Retention
Owner: S. Balaji
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0101-R1
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0103-R0
Related Forms:

● CORP-QA-FRM-1002-R0 (Record Retrieval Request)
Related Products/Equipment: All
1. Purpose
To ensure quality records are maintained, stored, protected, retrievable, and retained for
defined periods.
2. Scope
All GxP records including batch records, lab data, deviation/CAPA records, training,
calibration, maintenance, audits, and complaints.
3. Responsibilities
● Record Owners: Ensure completeness and accuracy.
● QA Document Control: Archival, retention schedule, retrieval process.
● IT: Backup, security, and access control for electronic records.
● All Users: Proper handling and legibility of records.
4. Procedure
4.1 Record Creation
1. Records created per approved templates/SOPs.
2. Entries must be contemporaneous, legible, and attributable.
4.2 Storage
1. Electronic records stored in validated systems with access control.
2. Paper records stored in designated, secure areas.
4.3 Retention
1. Retention periods defined per record type (e.g., batch records: product shelf life + 1 year
minimum).

2. QA maintains retention schedule.
4.4 Retrieval
1. Retrieval requests logged via CORP-QA-FRM-1002.
2. QA ensures controlled access and tracking.
4.5 Disposal
1. Obsolete records disposed per retention schedule.
2. Disposal recorded and approved by QA.
5. Related Records
● Retention Schedule (CORP-QA-MST-1120-R0)
● Record Retrieval Logs
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy
SOP 3
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0103-R0
Title: Good Documentation Practices for GxP Records
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0101-R1
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0119-R0

Related Products/Equipment: All
1. Purpose
To define expectations for creation, modification, and maintenance of GxP records to ensure
data integrity and ALCOA+ principles.
2. Scope
All GxP personnel creating or modifying records in paper or electronic form.
3. Responsibilities
● All Employees: Follow GDP in daily work.
● Managers: Ensure team compliance and training.
● QA: Oversight, audit, and escalation.
4. Procedure
4.1 General Requirements
1. Entries must be:
○ Attributable (who)
○ Legible (readable)
○ Contemporaneous (at time of activity)
○ Original (first record)
○ Accurate (correct)
○ Complete (no missing data)
○ Consistent, Enduring, Available (ALCOA+)
2. No use of pencils or erasable ink in controlled paper records.
4.2 Corrections
1. Single strike-through; original entry remains legible.
2. Add corrected value, date, initials, and reason.

4.3 Electronic Records
1. Use validated systems only.
2. No sharing of credentials.
3. Audit trails must not be disabled.
4.4 Signatures
1. Electronic signatures per approved system.
2. Handwritten signatures must be full and legible.
5. Related Records
● Data Integrity Procedure (CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0119-R0)
● Training records on GDP
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy
SOP 4
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0
Title: Deviation Management and Preliminary Event Triage
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0108-R0 (RCA)
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0109-R0 (CAPA)
Related Forms:

● CORP-QA-FRM-1001-R0 (Deviation Intake)
● CORP-QA-TMP-0002-R0 (Investigation Report Template)
Related Products/Equipment: All
1. Purpose
To define the process for detection, documentation, triage, and investigation of deviations
impacting product quality, compliance, or patient safety.
2. Scope
All GxP areas at PASH, VISK, and CORP.
3. Responsibilities
● Event Originator: Immediate notification and preliminary documentation.
● QA: Triage, classification, investigation assignment, oversight.
● Functional Owners: Investigation execution and CAPA implementation.
4. Procedure
4.1 Detection and Reporting
1. Any employee identifies deviation (process, equipment, documentation, system, etc.).
2. Record details in Deviation Intake Form within 24 hours.
4.2 Triage and Classification
1. QA reviews within 1 working day.
2. Classify as Critical, Major, or Minor based on risk.
4.3 Investigation
1. Assign investigator and due date.
2. Use CORP-QA-TMP-0002 for report.
3. Include impact assessment, root cause, and CAPA recommendations.

4.4 Closure
1. QA reviews investigation completeness.
2. Ensures CAPA initiation where required.
3. Approves closure.
5. Related Records
● Deviation Register (CORP-QA-MST-1104-R0)
● Linked CAPAs and Investigations
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy
SOP 5
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0108-R0
Title: Root Cause Analysis Methodology and Investigation Standards
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0109-R0
Related Forms:
● CORP-QA-FRM-1003-R0 (RCA Worksheet)

1. Purpose
To standardize root cause analysis (RCA) methods and investigation quality across Aetheris.
2. Scope
All investigations (deviations, OOS, complaints, data integrity events).
3. Responsibilities
● Investigators: Apply appropriate RCA tools.
● QA: Ensure investigation quality and consistency.
4. Procedure
4.1 RCA Tools
1. 5 Whys for straightforward issues.
2. Fishbone (Ishikawa) for multi-factor problems.
3. Fault Tree for complex technical failures.
4.2 Investigation Content
1. Problem statement.
2. Timeline.
3. Data collection.
4. Root cause identification.
5. Impact assessment.
6. CAPA recommendations.
4.3 Documentation
1. Use CORP-QA-FRM-1003 for RCA.
2. Attach evidence (logs, interviews, data prints).
5. Related Records
● Investigation Reports

● CAPA Files
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy
SOP 6
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0109-R0
Title: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Management
Owner: Meera Iyer
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0110-R0
Related Forms:
● CORP-QA-FRM-1004-R0 (CAPA Initiation)
● CORP-QA-FRM-1005-R0 (Effectiveness Review)
1. Purpose
To define the CAPA lifecycle from initiation through effectiveness verification.
2. Scope
All CAPAs arising from deviations, complaints, audits, OOS, and other quality events.

3. Responsibilities
● CAPA Owner: Action implementation.
● QA: CAPA approval, tracking, effectiveness review coordination.
4. Procedure
4.1 Initiation
1. CAPA raised via CORP-QA-FRM-1004.
2. Define problem, root cause, actions, due dates, owners.
4.2 Implementation
1. Execute actions as planned.
2. Document evidence of completion.
4.3 Effectiveness Check
1. Define metric and time window at initiation.
2. Review data post-implementation.
3. Record result in CORP-QA-FRM-1005.
4.4 Closure
1. QA verifies all actions and effectiveness evidence.
2. Approves closure.
5. Related Records
● CAPA Register (CORP-QA-MST-1103-R0)
● Linked Investigations
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy

SOP 7
Document ID: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0119-R0
Title: Handling of Data Integrity Incidents and Audit Trail Review
Owner: P. Singh
Site: CORP
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0118-R0 (Data Integrity Governance)
Related Forms:
● CORP-QA-FRM-1011-R0 (DI Incident Form)
1. Purpose
To define detection, escalation, and investigation of data integrity (DI) incidents and periodic
audit trail review.
2. Scope
All GxP electronic systems (QMS, LIMS, MES, ERP, LMS).
3. Responsibilities
● System Owners: Ensure audit trail enabled.
● IT: Technical support and access control.
● QA: DI oversight and investigation.
4. Procedure
4.1 DI Incident Detection
1. Via audit trail, anomaly, or whistleblower.

2. Log in CORP-QA-FRM-1011.
4.2 Escalation
1. QA assesses risk and notifies CQO.
2. Initiate deviation if product impact possible.
4.3 Audit Trail Review
1. Periodic review per system risk.
2. Document findings and actions.
5. Related Records
● DI Incident Logs
● Audit Trail Review Records
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Dr. Suresh Reddy
SOP 8
Document ID: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0301-R0
Title: Gowning Qualification and Entry into Sterile Core Areas
Owner: T. Srinivas
Site: VISK
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related SOPs:
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0302-R0 (EM)
● VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0303-R0 (Cleaning/Disinfection)

Related Products: FPP-2001/2002/2003
Related Equipment: All sterile core equipment
1. Purpose
To define gowning qualification and entry controls for classified sterile areas.
2. Scope
All personnel entering Grade A/B/C/D areas at VISK.
3. Responsibilities
● Employees: Adhere to gowning procedure.
● QA/Production: Qualification and oversight.
4. Procedure
1. Gowning steps (hand wash, sterile gown, gloves, mask, goggles).
2. Qualification via media contact plates and visual check.
3. Requalification frequency (annual or after breach).
5. Related Records
● Gowning Qualification Records
● EM Data
6. Revision History
Version Effective Date Changes Approved By
R0 2026-07-11 Initial release Meera Iyer

This completes Phase 0.5B – Core SOPs (01–20).
Next, I can generate:
● SOPs 21–40 (more OSD, API, QC, warehouse, RA SOPs), or
● Start detailed batch records (e.g., 10–15 key BMRs including the incoming event batch),
or
● Start detailed investigation/CAPA packages for key historical events.
Tell me which you want next.
